# Explore PDF and e-signature plans for business.

Keep your business moving with Acrobat, the all-in-one document productivity solution for editing, sharing, signing, and more — anytime, anywhere.

Best value

Acrobat Standard for teams

US$16.99/moper license

_Annual, billed monthly_

The simple PDF app for editing and converting documents, plus admin features.

[Buy now](https://commerce.adobe.com/store/segmentation?cli=doc_cloud&ctx=if&co=US&lang=en&ms=COM&ot=BASE&cs=t&pa=acro_direct_team&rtc=t&lo=sl&af=uc_new_user_iframe%2Cuc_new_system_close)

Acrobat Pro for teams

US$23.99/moper license

_Annual, billed monthly. Up to 10 licenses with free trial._

**Save 7.5% your first year with 3 or more licenses.**

The highly secure PDF and e-signature solution with advanced tools, plus admin features.

[Free trial](https://commerce.adobe.com/store/segmentation?cli=doc_cloud&ctx=if&co=US&lang=en&ms=COM&ot=TRIAL&cs=t&pa=apcc_direct_indirect_team&rtc=t&lo=sl&af=uc_new_user_iframe%2Cuc_new_system_close) [Buy now](https://commerce.adobe.com/store/segmentation?cli=doc_cloud&ctx=if&co=US&lang=en&ms=COM&ot=BASE&cs=t&pa=apcc_direct_indirect_team&rtc=t&lo=sl&af=uc_new_user_iframe%2Cuc_new_system_close)

Best value

Acrobat Studio for teams

US$29.99/moper license

_Annual, billed monthly. Up to 10 licenses with free trial._

**Save 7.5% your first year with 3 or more licenses.**

Everything in Acrobat Pro plus PDF Spaces, Acrobat AI Assistant, Adobe Express Premium, and admin features.

[Free trial](https://commerce.adobe.com/store/segmentation?cli=doc_cloud&ctx=if&co=US&lang=en&ms=COM&ot=TRIAL&cs=t&pa=PA-1807&rtc=t&lo=sl&af=uc_new_user_iframe%2Cuc_new_system_close) [Buy now](https://commerce.adobe.com/store/segmentation?cli=doc_cloud&ctx=if&co=US&lang=en&ms=COM&ot=BASE&cs=t&pa=PA-1807&rtc=t&lo=sl&af=uc_new_user_iframe%2Cuc_new_system_close)

Edit text and images in a PDF

Create, fill, sign, and send forms easily

Collect signatures

Get admin tools to manage teams plus 24x7 support

## What’s included in Acrobat plans for business.

![](https://www.adobe.com/dc-shared/assets/images/business/pricing-plans/icon-license-mgmt-white.svg)

### Easy license management

Add, manage, and assign licenses in a single admin dashboard, and get simplified billing under one contract.

![](https://www.adobe.com/dc-shared/assets/images/business/pricing-plans/icon-expert-help-white.svg)

### Expert help whenever you need it

Access 24x7 support with dedicated chat plus 1:1 sessions with Acrobat product experts (2 per user per year).

![](https://www.adobe.com/dc-shared/assets/images/business/pricing-plans/icon-assets-white.svg)

### Company ownership of assets

Make sure documents and assets stay within the company, even when someone leaves.

![](https://www.adobe.com/dc-shared/assets/images/business/pricing-plans/icon-security-compliance-white.svg)

### Document security and compliance

Protect your IP with PDF security features. And ensure that your files work for everyone with the built-in Accessibility Assistant.

![](https://www.adobe.com/dc-shared/assets/images/business/pricing-plans/icon-access-anywhere-white.svg)

### Access anywhere

View on desktop, mobile, or in your web browser.

![](https://www.adobe.com/dc-shared/assets/images/business/pricing-plans/icon-tutorials-white.svg)

### Tutorials

Learn the basics or refine your skills with tutorials designed to inspire.

## Navigating eSignature APIs: A Developer's Guide to Rate Limits and Costs

In the rapidly evolving digital contract landscape, developers integrating eSignature solutions into applications face critical decisions around API performance and pricing. As businesses increasingly rely on electronic signatures for efficiency and compliance, understanding the technical constraints and financial implications of platforms like Adobe Acrobat Sign becomes essential. This article examines API rate limits and overage fees from a business perspective, highlighting how these factors influence scalability, cost management, and integration strategies for developers building or enhancing SaaS products, enterprise workflows, or custom applications.

## Adobe Acrobat Sign API: Rate Limits and Overage Fees Explained

For developers working with Adobe Acrobat Sign, API rate limits are a foundational aspect of ensuring reliable, scalable integrations. Adobe structures its API under the Adobe Sign REST API framework, which enforces throttling to maintain service stability and prevent abuse. From a commercial viewpoint, these limits balance developer needs with Adobe's infrastructure costs, encouraging efficient usage while protecting against overload during peak demands like high-volume contract signing in sales cycles.

The core rate limits for Adobe Acrobat Sign API vary by endpoint and plan tier, typically measured in requests per minute (RPM) or per day. For standard developer accounts on the free or basic tiers, the default limit is around 100 API calls per minute for most endpoints, such as creating agreements or retrieving signer status. However, for production environments, enterprise plans can negotiate higher thresholds—up to 1,000 RPM or more—based on volume commitments. These limits apply globally but can be influenced by regional data centers; for instance, in the US and EU, where Adobe maintains robust infrastructure, limits are more consistently enforced compared to emerging markets.

Overage fees kick in when developers exceed these predefined quotas, adding a layer of predictability to budgeting. Adobe's model is usage-based for API interactions, with overages billed per excess envelope or transaction. On the Developer tier (free for testing), there's no overage tolerance—exceeding limits results in temporary throttling or 429 errors, halting requests until the window resets. For paid plans like Team or Enterprise, overages are charged at approximately $0.50 to $1.00 per additional envelope beyond the base quota, depending on the agreement type (e.g., simple vs. complex with fields). In high-volume scenarios, such as API-driven bulk sends for HR onboarding, developers might face cumulative costs if not monitored via Adobe's API usage dashboard.

Business implications are significant: rate limits encourage optimization, like batching requests or using webhooks for asynchronous updates instead of polling. Overages can inflate operational expenses unexpectedly; for a mid-sized SaaS provider processing 10,000 envelopes monthly, breaching limits by 20% could add $1,000+ in fees. Adobe mitigates this with tools like the API rate limit header in responses, allowing developers to implement backoff strategies. In regions like the EU, where eIDAS regulations mandate secure, auditable eSignatures, Adobe's limits align with compliance needs by prioritizing verified transactions, but developers must factor in potential delays during verification spikes.

From an observational standpoint, Adobe's approach favors established enterprises with predictable workloads, but startups may find the rigidity challenging. Integration with Adobe's ecosystem, including PDF tools, adds value, yet the fee structure underscores the need for upfront quota assessments during planning.

![image](https://static.esignglobal.com/20251127_1941_Adobe_simple_compose_01kb2httnmftqttna4mq0d3mrb_3366684d27.png)

## DocuSign API: Rate Limits, Quotas, and Developer Considerations

DocuSign, a market leader in eSignature solutions, offers a robust Developer API platform with tiered plans that directly address rate limits and overages for scalable integrations. Drawing from official 2025 pricing, DocuSign's API is designed for developers embedding signing workflows into apps, with quotas tied to envelope volumes rather than pure request counts. This envelope-centric model—where an "envelope" represents a document package with signers—provides clarity for business forecasting.

Rate limits are enforced per account and endpoint: the Starter plan ($600/year) caps at about 40 envelopes per month, translating to roughly 1,000-2,000 API calls daily to avoid throttling. Intermediate ($3,600/year) supports 100 envelopes monthly with higher RPM (up to 300 for sends), while Advanced ($5,760/year) includes bulk send capabilities without strict per-call limits but with overall volume caps. Enterprise plans are custom, often exceeding 1,000 envelopes monthly with dedicated support. Limits reset monthly, and exceeding them triggers 429 errors, requiring exponential backoff.

Overage fees are metered and plan-dependent: for non-Enterprise tiers, excess envelopes cost $0.10-$0.50 each, billed post-month-end. In API-heavy use cases like automated contract generation, this can accumulate; a developer surpassing quotas by 50 envelopes might incur $25-$50 extra. DocuSign's dashboard offers real-time monitoring, and features like Connect Webhooks reduce polling needs, indirectly easing limit pressures. For global operations, US-based limits apply uniformly, but APAC users note latency impacts on real-time API responses.

Commercially, DocuSign's structure suits high-automation enterprises, with add-ons like identity verification incurring separate metered fees ($0.50+ per check). Developers benefit from extensive SDKs, but the seat-based licensing in core plans (e.g., $300/user/year for Standard) can compound API costs for team integrations.

What are the standard API rate limits for Adobe Acrobat Sign developers?

Adobe Acrobat Sign imposes rate limits on API calls to ensure system stability, typically allowing up to 1,000 API requests per hour per user account, with variations based on the subscription tier. How are overage fees applied when exceeding Adobe Acrobat Sign API rate limits?

## Key Takeaways

- Adobe Sign pricing starts at about $12.99/mo for Acrobat Standard, $19.99/mo for Acrobat Pro, and Acrobat Pro for Teams at $23.99/month and Acrobat studio for Teams at $29.99/mo.The Enterprise tier is priced via custom quotes so you can pick a basic individual plan or scale up to enterprise-grade security and integrations as needs grow.
- Many advanced features of Adobe Sign (integrations, brand customization, compliance) live in higher tiers or as add-ons, and total cost can climb fast once you need PDF editing, API access, or 24/7 support.
- If your business relies heavily on PDFs, Adobe is a great fit. It offers deep Acrobat integration and advanced PDF editing tools. You also get powerful workflows like bulk sending and document routing. The platform ensures enterprise-grade compliance with HIPAA, FERPA, and GLBA. Plus, it integrates seamlessly with CRMs and other business platforms for complex document ecosystems.
- If you’re looking for a simpler, more budget-friendly end-to-end contract management solution that still covers essentials (unlimited templates, easy integrations, solid security, responsive support), Signeasy is an excellent Adobe Sign alternative.
- For many organizations, the real cost of Adobe Sign was tied to how deeply it was integrated into existing tools. For SharePoint‑centric teams, Adobe Acrobat Sign support for the SharePoint Online integration ended on June 28, 2024. Since then, the SharePoint plug‑in has no longer supported sending new agreements, and completed documents have stopped flowing back into SharePoint libraries automatically.

| Tool | Starting Price | Key Strength | Limitation |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Adobe Sign | $12.99/mo | PDF editing + signing, Adobe ecosystem | Compliance features only in Enterprise |
| Signeasy | $10/user/mo | Easy setup, unlimited docs, HIPAA ready | Smaller integration library |
| Docusign | $10/user/mo | Global recognition, robust compliance | Expensive at scale, per-envelope fees |
| PandaDoc | $19/user/mo | Proposal + contract creation | Complex for simple eSign needs |
| Dropbox Sign | $20/mo | Simple UI, Dropbox native | Limited templates on lower plans |

[Start Free Trial](https://app.signeasy.com/signup)

Adobe Sign pricing starts at $12.99/month for Acrobat Standard (individual) and scales to $29.99/month per user for Acrobat Studio for Teams, with Enterprise plans available at custom pricing. All plans include PDF editing and basic e-signature tools, while advanced features like bulk sending, custom branding, AI Assistant, and enterprise compliance (HIPAA, FERPA) require higher-tier plans. Annual billing is required, with a 7-day free trial available for Acrobat Pro.

When it comes to eSignature solutions, Adobe Acrobat Sign is one of the most well-established platforms. However, with a wide range of pricing plans and feature-packed tools, it can be tough to determine if Adobe Sign is the right choice for your business.

Choosing between eSignature platforms can be challenging, especially since Acrobat Sign offers more than just eSignatures. With additional features like PDF editing, payment collection, and integrations with everyday productivity tools, Adobe Sign has positioned itself as a multifunctional solution for enterprises in the U.S. and around the world.

In this article, we'll break down Adobe Sign’s pricing, compare its features to competitors in the market, and help you decide if it’s the best fit for your organization.

[![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/5f83f30d99580b736213bc4e/67ea4fb96d050e126e943256_Adobe%20sign%20pricing%20comparison.webp)](https://signeasy.com/contact-sales)

**Things to keep in mind:**

- Adobe Sign offers customized pricing plans for individuals, small businesses, and enterprise-level organizations.
- Adobe Sign plans include Adobe’s built-in PDF editing capabilities, a key differentiator in the market.
- Transaction limits and tiered security features could pose limitations, depending on the size and needs of your organization.

## How we sourced our data on Adobe Sign pricing

To give you the clearest picture of Adobe Sign pricing, we pulled our data from several reliable sources. We started by diving into Adobe Sign’s official pricing pages to get the most up-to-date information on their plans.

We also looked at what real users are saying about the pricing and value through customer reviews, and gathered insights from industry reports to see how Adobe Sign’s pricing stacks up against competitors.

On top of that, we did our own research, exploring the platform to better understand how each pricing tier translates into features and benefits. Our goal is to help you make an informed decision about whether Adobe Sign’s pricing fits your business needs.

## Acrobat Sign pricing overview

Adobe Sign provides pricing options that cater to different user needs, from individuals and small businesses to large enterprises. Here's an overview of the available plans:

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/5f83f30d99580b736213bc4e/66fbbaa8cd915986eb0812be_AD_4nXdMLjWvGWf_A_ndjbkn_9A6RDO7r7l_R4LrmS7LbthBszRDlk5H0RMSyHuaeZfTlpXYXDX9OihWl66mVdVqKbPLNJFCv2iMz0wRFTumSQk8zqX75ApTa-Dh2DT3zIxpqOiZRnDSi4kYveUwtNZSe01Yuls.webp)

Adobe Sign pricing plans for Individuals

### 1\. Adobe Acrobat Standard

- Cost: $12.99/month (annual plan)
- Features: Basic eSignature capabilities, PDF editing, integration with Microsoft 365, and limited form creation tools.
- Best for: Individuals and small businesses handling low to moderate eSignature workflows.

### 2\. Adobe Acrobat Pro

- Cost: $19.99/month (annual plan)
- Features: Includes everything in the Standard plan, plus custom branding, payment collection tools, bulk sending options, and access to Adobe Document Cloud for cloud-based document management.
- Best for: Businesses needing a more comprehensive eSignature workflow with branding and payment integration.

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/5f83f30d99580b736213bc4e/66fbbaa7b27a1068896db3eb_AD_4nXea9GpgyQjeVyHaRigF3SlpLwdVB290nLdmVFbXn4pIimBVOsDCqUzOHnq-svG4-OsFrfX8rmMhLAhJEtm5ByV9Lz_iIdu88pzO3zotGcGnniPQ60NmNK7vRTHZ_diHDFOIHfRInuXocYL1yA2LcOg7kAo.webp)

Adobe Sign pricing plans for Businesses

### 3\. Acrobat Standard for Teams

- Cost: $14.99/month per user (annual plan)
- Features: User and group management via Admin Console, account delegation, PDF editing, and eSigning tools. This plan is designed for teams with capped transactions (150 per user, per year).
- Best for: Teams that need basic eSignature tools and basic user management but can manage with limited transactions.

### 4\. Acrobat Pro for Teams

- Cost: $23.99/month per user (annual plan)
- Features: All features from the individual Pro plan, plus custom branding, advanced form creation, and 24/7 customer support for quick issue resolution.
- Best for: Teams looking for robust document workflows and round-the-clock customer support.

### 5\. **Acrobat Studio for Teams**

- Cost: $29.99/month per user (annual plan)
- Features: Everything in Acrobat Pro for Teams, plus Acrobat AI Assistant, PDF Spaces for collaborative knowledge hubs, Adobe Express Premium for content creation. Launched August 2025 as Adobe's most advanced Acrobat offering.
- Best for: Teams needing AI-powered document analysis, collaborative workspaces, and creative content tools alongside PDF and eSignature workflows.

### 6\. Adobe Acrobat Sign Enterprise

- Cost: Custom pricing
- Features: Enhanced security and compliance features (including HIPAA, FERPA, GLBA compliance), CRM integration (Salesforce, Workday), API access for embedded signing, and advanced authentication options. Additional features like PDF editing can be added at extra cost.
- Best for: Large enterprises that need high-level security and integrations with CRM tools but may require additional features like PDF editing as paid add-ons.

When considering Adobe Sign pricing, there are several important details to keep in mind, especially around add-ons, user requirements, and payment options:

For teams using Acrobat Standard and Acrobat Pro plans, Adobe offers an AI Assistant add-on, available at an **additional cost of $4.99 per month**. This feature can enhance your document workflow by providing AI-driven assistance, making document management more efficient.

The Acrobat Standard for Teams and Acrobat Pro for Teams plans come with a minimum user requirement of two licenses. This means that **teams must purchase at least two licenses**, which could increase the overall cost for smaller teams. The pricing is listed as per user, so larger teams should account for multiple licenses in their overall budget.

A free trial option is available for certain plans, such as Acrobat Pro for individuals and Acrobat Pro for Teams. This trial allows users to experience the platform before committing to a paid plan. However, it's worth noting that not **all plans offer a free trial** — for example, the Acrobat Standard for Teams plan does not include this option. Be sure to check the availability of a free trial based on the plan you're interested in.

Adobe Sign offers two annual payment options: Annual, paid monthly and Annual, prepaid. With the **Annual, paid monthly** option, you commit to an annual subscription but pay on a monthly basis. You can cancel within 14 days for a full refund, but if you cancel after 14 days, a fee applies. The **Annual, prepaid** option requires you to pay the entire subscription cost upfront. If you cancel within 14 days, you’re eligible for a full refund, but no refund is offered if you cancel after 14 days. For teams deeply embedded in Salesforce, [Conga Sign](https://signeasy.com/blog/business/conga-sign-pricing) is another option worth comparing for native CRM integration.

[![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/5f83f30d99580b736213bc4e/67513b3579c1b0ad4541a1be_67513af54a8db4ba77be9c1d_Blog%2520CTA%2520Banners-%2520Pricing.webp)](https://signeasy.com/pricing)

### Pricing insights from user reviews

User reviews of Adobe Acrobat Sign often highlight that the platform can be slightly more expensive than other eSignature solutions. However, many users feel that the extra cost is justified by its advanced features and strong security.

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/5f83f30d99580b736213bc4e/66fbbaa780fbb81eaeec6c9d_AD_4nXcFjAmRIrK9UnPmgsaUWe4WP-PFuOpdvg7clflO9DIvb_YdW_sRnsGlZ-8hEnb7uawIjHmNyh6Zhw188DxP9wNKH6tSkKiuBR-UwklW-tqIGMaIt1NP9aqAJmX6WsVVDKHAKp9Ag_kgDhE17Ll1LFrI4g.webp)

[Adobe Sign pricing review by a user on G2](https://www.g2.com/products/adobe-acrobat-sign/reviews/adobe-acrobat-sign-review-8175014)

Businesses with higher document volumes find the pricing more competitive, while smaller organizations might notice the cost more. For enterprises, the platform’s compliance capabilities and integrations provide additional value, but for small businesses or infrequent users, the pricing may not be as convenient or cost-effective.

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/5f83f30d99580b736213bc4e/66fbbaa684806d882bb43971_AD_4nXcLgNCkZADizgJg7qmX5t2PdC2reR8SecJB2ZLDqrAzS37o45R8s75YUktyAZVSohBd85Z-x9Wqrs0tBsfs0exQ0VXSU5M6VZZ4ZtLpkOU6vsYy3qxAAcQ_FGQQkRBPOVBKCb23n1SlTPwVnm-g02VWJPw.webp)

[Adobe Sign pricing review by a user on Trust Radius](https://www.trustradius.com/reviews/adobe-acrobat-sign-2024-06-27-13-24-35)

## **Key Considerations Before Choosing an Adobe Sign Plan**

- Billing: Annual plans (paid monthly) offer lowest rates. Month-to-month is higher (e.g., Pro: $29.99/mo vs. $19.99/mo annual).
- Transaction limits: Individual plans = unlimited. Team plans cap at 150/user/year. Contact Adobe for custom limits.
- Free trial: 7-day trial for Acrobat Pro (individual + teams). Not available for Standard for Teams.
- Minimum licenses: Team plans require minimum 2 licenses. Teams of 5+ may get 7.5% volume discount.
- Hidden costs: AI Assistant add-on = $4.99/mo extra. HIPAA/FERPA compliance only in Enterprise at custom pricing.

‍

## Adobe Sign plans and features: The possible pitfalls

When it comes to eSignature solutions, the landscape is competitive, with platforms like Signeasy, Docusign, PandaDoc, and HelloSign providing alternatives. If you're evaluating Docusign alongside Adobe Sign, our [Docusign pricing](https://signeasy.com/blog/business/docusign-reviews) guide can help you compare costs directly. Here are some possible pitfalls when it comes to Acrobat Sign.

### Transaction limits

One area where Adobe Sign may not be ideal for every business is transaction limits. While individual plans offer “unlimited transactions,” team plans cap usage at 150 transactions per user/year. Competitors of Adobe Sign provide more flexibility, particularly for high-volume businesses, by offering customizable transaction limits.

### Security and compliance

Adobe Sign provides high-level security, with enterprise plans ensuring compliance with industry standards such as HIPAA and GLBA. However, it’s important to note that only the enterprise plan includes these enhanced compliance features. Competitors of Adobe Sign, like Signeasy, also offer strong security and compliance, but without the segmentation between individual and enterprise plans. This makes those competitors gain a strategic advantage over Adobe Sign to be relevant for smaller organizations seeking top-level security.

### Important for SharePoint users

If your contract workflows depended on Adobe Sign’s SharePoint integrations, the end of service has already become a critical factor in your eSignature strategy.

After June 28, 2024, the Acrobat Sign SharePoint Online plug‑in stopped working for new agreements, and completed documents no longer save automatically to SharePoint.

Rebuilding these workflows in other tools, training teams, and managing manual workarounds adds hidden operational costs that go well beyond the subscription price.

For SharePoint‑first organizations, evaluating alternatives like [Signeasy—which offers a live SharePoint integration](https://signeasy.com/integrations/sharepoint)—helps avoid ongoing disruption and overhead.

## Features breakdown by plan

Acrobat Sign’s plans are structured to meet different business needs, with a variety of features available across tiers. When you're deciding which Adobe Sign plan to choose, it's important to understand how the features vary across plans and how that affects the pricing.

Let’s see what each plan offers and where you might need to upgrade depending on your business needs.

### Basic features for all plans

No matter which plan you choose, you’ll have access to essential eSignature tools.

## Compare all features

| E-signature core features | Acrobat Standard for Teams | Acrobat Pro for Teams | Acrobat Sign |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Interact with your document in the desktop or web app for quick answers and one-click summaries | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Collect signatures | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Sign agreements | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Scan and sign on mobile | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Track and manage agreements | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Add a business stamp | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Prepare forms | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Customize languages | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Keep records and audit trails | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Create and share reusable templates | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Get 24x7 product support | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Add customized branding | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Collect e-signatures on websites | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Collect payments with Braintree | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Advanced forms | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |
| Send in bulk | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Advanced language support | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |
| Advanced roles and routing | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |
| Create consistent processes with Workflow Designer | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |

These basic features include:

- Collecting signatures and signing agreements – the fundamental purpose of any eSignature platform.
- Scan and sign on mobile – flexibility to sign documents on the go.
- Track and manage agreements – keep an eye on document progress with real-time tracking.
- Prepare forms and customize languages – basic form creation and language support.
- Keep records and audit trails – ensures transparency and traceability for your signed documents.

For smaller teams or businesses just starting with eSignatures, these features are more than enough to manage straightforward signing workflows. You’re getting everything you need to collect signatures and manage agreements, all while staying mobile and efficient.

### Advanced features (Available in Pro and Enterprise plans)

However, if your business has more complex needs, you might find that these advanced features, available in Pro and Enterprise plans, are worth the upgrade:

- Custom branding – personalize your documents with your own logo and brand elements.
- Bulk sending – save time by sending documents to multiple recipients at once.
- Collect payments with Braintree – easily integrate payment collection into your signing workflows.
- Advanced roles and routing – ensure documents are sent to the right people with advanced workflows.
- 24/7 customer support – get assistance whenever you need it, a crucial feature for larger teams that depend on quick issue resolution.

When it comes to security and compliance, the Enterprise plan takes things up a notch with advanced security certifications, industry-specific compliance (like HIPAA and FERPA), and enhanced authentication options. These features are especially important for businesses in highly regulated industries where compliance is non-negotiable. Lower-tier plans won’t have these, so if compliance is a top concern, be prepared to invest in a higher plan.

|  | Acrobat Standard for Teams | Acrobat Pro for Teams | Acrobat Sign |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Built-in integrations | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |
| APIs | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Additional pre-built integrations | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |
| Admin Features | - |
| Manage users with Admin Console | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Manage groups | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Account delegation | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |

Next up is admin features. As your team grows, managing users and permissions becomes more important. Features like Admin Console, group management, and account delegation are essential for larger teams. These tools are available in the Team and Enterprise plans, making them ideal for businesses managing multiple users. The Standard plans, while cost-effective, don't offer advanced admin support or 24/7 customer service, so if your team needs more control and quicker support, upgrading to a higher plan makes sense.

For those looking to integrate Adobe Sign with other platforms, the Pro and Enterprise plans come with built-in integrations for tools like Microsoft 365, Salesforce, Workday, and ServiceNow. If API access is a priority for your team, explore our detailed guide on the [adobe sign api](https://signeasy.com/esign-api-guide/adobe-esign-api) to understand its capabilities, limitations, and how it compares to alternatives.

These integrations allow you to streamline workflows and connect eSignatures with the platforms your teams already use. If you need even deeper customization, the Enterprise plan also offers API access and additional pre-built integrations, making it a good fit for businesses that require complex, multi-tool ecosystems.

Additionally, if you’re working with a lot of PDFs, the Pro and Enterprise plans give you access to advanced PDF editing tools. From creating fillable forms to redacting sensitive information and validating PDFs for ISO and accessibility standards, these tools make managing documents easier and more secure.

## PDF Features Comparison

| PDF Features | Acrobat Standard for Teams | Acrobat Pro for Teams | Acrobat Sign |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| View, share, comment on, and track PDFs for review | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Create PDFs and export to Word, Excel, or PowerPoint | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Export PDFs to Word, PPT, Excel, or JPG, TIFF, PNG formats | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Create a single PDF from multiple files | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Convert documents and images to high-quality PDFs | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Edit PDFs, add or replace text and images | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Protect PDFs with passwords | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Turn paper or Word files into fillable forms | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Turn scanned documents into searchable PDFs | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Compare two versions of a document | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Create and validate PDFs to meet accessibility standards | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Redact to permanently remove sensitive information | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Validate and fix PDFs for ISO and accessibility standards | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |

These added functionalities make these plans more expensive, but they’re necessary for businesses with advanced document needs.

## Adobe Sign support

Adobe Sign offers varying levels of customer support depending on the plan.

While basic support is available in the lower-tier plans, only the Pro and Enterprise plans provide 24/7 customer support, ensuring you can get assistance anytime you need it. This is especially valuable for larger teams or businesses that rely heavily on the platform and can’t afford delays.

For users on Standard plans, support is more limited, so upgrading is worth considering if quick and reliable assistance is a priority for your business.

## How to choose the right plan

When selecting an Acrobat Sign plan, the choice largely depends on the size of your business and the complexity of your document workflows.

The pricing of Adobe Sign plans reflects the features they include. If your business has complex workflows, requires strict security, or handles high document volumes, you’ll likely need to invest in a higher-tier plan to obtain the functionality you require.

On the flip side, if you’re a smaller operation with simpler needs, the basic plans provide solid eSignature tools without breaking the bank. The Acrobat Pro plan offers great value at $19.99/month, with PDF editing, payment collection, and bulk sending.

## Adobe Sign Vs Signeasy: An in-depth comparison

When choosing between Adobe Sign and Signeasy, it’s essential to look at how they differ in pricing, plan structure, features, and support to determine the best fit for your needs.

| Category | Signeasy | Adobe Sign |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Starting Price (Individual Plan)** | $10/month | $12.99/month (Standard), $19.99/month (Pro) |
| **Free Plan Available** | Yes | No |
| **Free Trial** | 14-day free trial (no card required) | Available only on select plans |
| **Unlimited Templates** | Yes (even in paid basic plans) | No |
| **Integrations (Google Workspace, HubSpot, Teams, Outlook)** | Available across plans | Only available in higher-tier plans |
| **Advanced Authentication (Passcode, Biometric)** | Included | Only in Enterprise plan |
| **Bulk Sending** | Included in base paid plan | Only in Pro and Enterprise plans |
| **Custom Branding** | Available in all paid plans | Only in Pro and above |

## Frequently asked questions

Is Adobe Sign free to use?

No, Adobe Sign is not free. It offers a free trial for new users, but after that, users must choose from various paid plans tailored to individuals, teams, or enterprises.

Does Adobe Sign charge per document?

Adobe Sign does not charge per document in the traditional sense. The plans have specific caps in terms of the number of documents per month. Businesses may want to consider their volume needs when selecting a plan.

Is Adobe Sign worth it?

Whether Adobe Sign is worth it depends on your specific needs. It offers robust features, including seamless PDF editing and extensive integrations, making it a valuable option for businesses that heavily use PDFs and require advanced document management. However, for companies with high-volume transaction needs or those that prioritize flexibility, other competitors might offer more suitable solutions. Competitor platforms like Signeasy can be a viable alternative to Adobe Sign.

What are Adobe Sign's pricing plans?

Adobe Sign offers 5 main plans: Adobe Acrobat Standard ($12.99/month), Adobe Acrobat Pro ($19.99/month), Acrobat Standard for Teams ($14.99/month per user), Acrobat Pro for Teams ($23.99/month per user), and Adobe Acrobat Sign Enterprise (custom pricing).

Does Adobe Sign offer a free plan?

Adobe Sign doesn't offer a free plan, but it does provide a free trial for certain plans like Acrobat Pro for individuals and Acrobat Pro for Teams. However, not all plans include a free trial option.

Are there transaction limits on Adobe Sign plans?

Yes, individual plans offer \\"unlimited transactions,\\" but team plans cap usage at 150 transactions per user per year. This can be a limitation for high-volume businesses."

What's the minimum user requirement for team plans?

The Acrobat Standard for Teams and Acrobat Pro for Teams plans come with a minimum user requirement of two licenses, which could increase costs for smaller teams.

Does Adobe Sign still work with SharePoint?

Adobe Acrobat Sign has discontinued its SharePoint Online integration; service ended on June 28, 2024. Since then, you cannot use the plug‑in to send new agreements from SharePoint, and completed documents are no longer saved automatically to SharePoint libraries. For SharePoint on‑premises environments, Adobe has also ended service for the Acrobat Sign integration, so customers have had to migrate those workflows to other tools or platforms. If you want to keep your eSignature workflows inside SharePoint, Signeasy provides a dedicated SharePoint add‑in that allows you to create, send, track, and manage signatures from within SharePoint.
